svg.text: All 9 Decoupling Boundaries & Why They Exist

g.text: SOURCE RING (4096 slots)
Multi-egress isolation: Different consumers (RTMP, SRT, HLS, Recording) run at independent rates
Cannot run end-to-end: One slow egress (network congestion, disk stall) must not block others
Cost: 1 ring, lock-free SPMC (Arc allocation per packet). Necessary for scalability.
TS CHUNK RING (per preset)
Shared mux result: SRT muxer produces one TsMuxer output, multiple SRT connections read it
Decouples egress producer from multiple egress consumers at different rates
Alternative: Per-connection muxing (inefficient, duplicates work per connection)
MEMORY QUEUE (SRT sender isolation)
Async ↔ Blocking boundary: libsrt_send() blocks indefinitely on network congestion
Cannot run on Tokio: Blocking one worker thread ≠ blocking all tasks (OS thread is isolated)
Backpressure: Full queue tells Tokio to slow down; empty queue blocks OS thread
Cost: 1 Condvar-based queue per SRT output. Non-negotiable for SRT protocol.
MEMORY QUEUE (FFmpeg stdin feeder)
Async ↔ Blocking boundary: FFmpeg subprocess is blocking, must run on OS thread
Input buffering: Feeder task (Tokio) writes TS packets into queue; subprocess reads continuously
Decouples Tokio ingest rate from FFmpeg decode rate (highly variable)
Cost: 1 queue per preset. Essential for transcoding to not block ingest.
OUTPUT RING (transcoded packets, per preset)
Multi-consumer fan-out: Transcoded output shared by multiple egress paths (RTMP, SRT, etc.)
Isolates FFmpeg stdout reader from multiple egress tasks at different rates
Cost: 1 ring per preset. Necessary for efficient transcoder sharing.
MEMORY QUEUE (H.265→H.264 codec)
Async ↔ Blocking boundary: libavcodec H.265→H.264 conversion blocks
Only for RTMP egress: RTMP protocol cannot carry H.265, must convert
Per-stage keying: Independent threads for (H.265→H.264:from:source) and (:from:720p)
Cost: 2 OS threads when both RTMP-src + RTMP-720p with H.265 ingest. Allows parallelization.
AUDIO ROUTING RINGS (per track config)
Multi-consumer isolation: Different egress outputs need different audio track selections
Could inline if only 1 output per config, but design is general for multi-output
Cost: 1 ring per audio configuration. Optional optimization: merge if single-output-per-config.
HLS SEGMENT STORE (Mutex)
Shared read/write: Segmenter writes completed segments; HTTP handlers read them
Low contention (~0.17 Hz = 1 lock per 6 seconds). BEST run-to-completion case.
Cost: 1 Mutex. Could use lock-free atomic swaps (if contention increases), but currently optimal.
MEMORY QUEUE (Recording disk writer)
Async ↔ Blocking boundary: fwrite() to disk blocks unpredictably (100+ ms stalls)
Not in hot path: Recording is optional feature. Per-recording queue isolates from Tokio.
Cost: 1 queue per active recording. Could use io-uring for async I/O (not worth complexity).
